concerns.text: Task T015 is listed twice with conflicting statuses and descriptions. First as a test task (T015 [P] [US1] Unit test for physical bond descriptor extraction) and later as an implementation task (T015 [US1] Implement QM9 download...). The implementation task for QM9 download (FR-001) is missing a unique ID or the test task ID is incorrect. This ambiguity prevents verification of FR-001 coverage.

Task T022 is defined as a test task ('Unit test for physical bond descriptor extraction') but is also defined as an implementation task ('Extract physical bond descriptors...'). The implementation task for FR-002 (3D coordinates, atom types, bond connectivity) is covered by T017, but the specific extraction of 'physical bond descriptors' (bond lengths/angles) mentioned in T022 is not explicitly mapped to a unique implementation task ID distinct from the test. The spec FR-002 does not explicitly mandate 'physical bond descriptors' (only coordinates/types/connectivity), making the coverage of this specific extension unclear.

Task T022 is reused as a test task for US2 ('Contract test for model_output schema'), but T022 was already used in Phase 3. Task IDs must be unique. Consequently, the contract test for model_output schema (required for FR-006/FR-011 validation) lacks a valid, unique task ID in the current list.

Task T023 is reused as a test task for US2 ('Integration test for GNN training pipeline'), but T023 was used in Phase 3. Task IDs must be unique. The integration test for GNN training (critical for FR-004/FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T024 is reused as a test task for US2 ('Integration test for Random Forest training pipeline'), but T024 was used in Phase 3. Task IDs must be unique. The integration test for RF training (critical for FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T025 is reused as a test task for US2 ('Unit test for MAE/RMSE metric computation'), but T025 was used in Phase 3. Task IDs must be unique. The unit test for metrics (critical for FR-006) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T026 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Implement SchNet-style GNN architecture'), but T026 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The implementation of the GNN (FR-004) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T027 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Implement Random Forest baseline'), but T027 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The implementation of the RF baseline (FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T028 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Implement GNN training...'), but T028 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The GNN training implementation (FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T029 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Train Random Forest baseline...'), but T029 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The RF training implementation (FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T030 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Implement identical train/test split...'), but T030 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The split implementation (FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T031 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Implement MAE and RMSE metric computation'), but T031 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The metric computation implementation (FR-006) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T032 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Compute MAE/RMSE against QM9...'), but T032 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The validation implementation (FR-011) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T033 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Save model checkpoints...'), but T033 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The checkpoint implementation (FR-005) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T034 is reused as an implementation task for US2 ('Generate results/metrics.csv...'), but T034 was used as a test task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The metrics report implementation (FR-012) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T035 is reused as a test task for US3 ('Integration test for permutation importance...'), but T035 was used as an implementation task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The permutation importance test (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T036 is reused as a test task for US3 ('Integration test for saliency mapping...'), but T036 was used as an implementation task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The saliency mapping test (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T037 is reused as a test task for US3 ('Unit test for paired t-test...'), but T037 was used as an implementation task in Phase 4. Task IDs must be unique. The t-test unit test (FR-008) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T038 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Implement permutation importance...'), but T038 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The permutation importance implementation (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T039 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Implement saliency mapping...'), but T039 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The saliency mapping implementation (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T040 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Rank structural contributions...'), but T040 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The ranking implementation (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T041 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Implement paired t-tests...'), but T041 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The t-test implementation (FR-008) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T042 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Generate results/attributions.json...'), but T042 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The attributions report implementation (FR-007) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T043 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Generate results/significance.csv...'), but T043 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The significance report implementation (FR-008) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T045 is reused as an implementation task for US3 ('Visualize feature-importance maps...'), but T045 was used as a test task in Phase 5. Task IDs must be unique. The visualization implementation (FR-009) lacks a valid, unique task ID.

Task T060 is defined as an implementation task for US3 ('Implement validation protocol comparison...') but is also marked as 'Removed - out-of-scope' in Phase 7. This creates a contradiction: the task exists in Phase 6 but is removed in Phase 7. If removed, FR-011 (validation against QM9) is not covered by a unique, active task. If active, the 'Removed' status in Phase 7 is incorrect.

Task T061 is defined as an implementation task for US3 ('Document hydration state limitations...') but is also marked as 'Removed - out-of-scope' in Phase 7. This creates a contradiction. If removed, the documentation requirement for limitations (Assumptions in spec.md) is not covered by a unique, active task.
Task T015 appears twice with different IDs (T015 test vs T015 implementation) and descriptions. This is a formatting error that breaks the task ID uniqueness constraint required for traceability.

Task IDs T022 through T034 are duplicated between the 'Tests for User Story 2' section and the 'Implementation for User Story 2' section. This breaks the unique ID requirement and makes it impossible to distinguish between test and implementation tasks for coverage analysis.

Task IDs T035 through T045 are duplicated between the 'Tests for User Story 3' section and the 'Implementation for User Story 3' section. This breaks the unique ID requirement and makes it impossible to distinguish between test and implementation tasks for coverage analysis.
Task T060 and T061 are listed in Phase 6 as active tasks but are marked as 'Removed - out-of-scope' in Phase 7. This inconsistency makes it unclear if these tasks are required for coverage or not.
